steps.description: Click the language selection button (flag icon) at the top-right corner of the VS Code welcome sign-in dialog.
Press Ctrl+Shift+~ to open a new terminal in Visual Studio Code, within the empty workspace view.
Type the text "cd ~/.vscode/extensions/teamsdevapp" into the Bash terminal input field in Visual Studio Code, within the workspace interface.
Press the Tab key in the integrated terminal of Visual Studio Code while typing the command `cd ~/.vscode/extensions/teamsdevapp` to trigger autocompletion.
Press the enter key in the VS Code Terminal after typing a command to execute it.
Type the command `code package.json` into the integrated Terminal panel at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code interface, within a workspace folder environment.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal after typing `code package.json` to open the package.json file.
Click the close (X) button at the top-right corner of the "Welcome to VS Code" sign-in dialog in the Visual Studio Code application to dismiss the dialog.
Open the Find bar in Visual Studio Code while editing the `package.json` file using the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+F.
Type text 'aikey' into the search input field of the Find dialog in Visual Studio Code, searching within the open package.json file.

@assertion When the folder name containing package.json does not include "alpha", the aiKey should start with "0c" and end with "55". When the folder name containing package.json does include "alpha", the aiKey should not start with "0c" and end with "55".
